title: Fatal: `_PyMem_DebugRawFree: bad ID` in `free_threadstate` (`pystate.c:1527`)
description: `Python/pystate.c`: a fresh free-threaded sub-interpreter hands out its embedded `_initial_thread` from `alloc_threadstate`; if the `Py_GIL_DISABLED`-only `_Py_qsbr_reserve`/`_Py_ReserveTLBCIndex` allocation fails under OOM, `free_threadstate` reads the still-NULL `tstate->base.interp`, so its `tstate == &interp->_initial_thread` check compares against a NULL-offset and is false, and it `PyMem_RawFree`s the interpreter-embedded (non-heap) thread state -> `_PyMem_DebugRawFree: bad ID` fatal (segfault on release)
filing_hold: Do not open an upstream python/cpython issue for now. Free-threaded sub-interpreter creation crash found by fuzzing -- de-prioritized by colesbury (FT lead) on python/cpython#143232: not worth fuzzing subinterpreters until the known FT issues are addressed (cf. python/cpython#129824). Already gisted (see gist_url) but never filed as an issue, and should stay that way for now. Revisit once the FT subinterpreter area stabilizes.
notes: Free-threading-specific: the failing _Py_qsbr_reserve/_Py_ReserveTLBCIndex reservations and the free_threadstate cleanup are gated on Py_GIL_DISABLED, so GIL builds (jit, upstream) never reach the path -> n/a (upstream still segfaults at a nearby start, but in an unrelated OOM site: make_unraisable_hook_args/PyStructSequence_New during Py_EndInterpreter, pylifecycle.c:2292). Root cause: init_interpreter (pystate.c:568) publishes interp->threads.preallocated = &interp->_initial_thread but never sets _initial_thread.base.interp; alloc_threadstate (L1498) hands that out; on OOM reservation failure new_threadstate calls free_threadstate (L1668/L1673), which reads the still-NULL tstate->base.interp (only set later in init_threadstate L1558), so tstate == &interp->_initial_thread (L1520) compares against &((NULL)->_initial_thread) and is false, and PyMem_RawFree(tstate) at L1527 frees the interpreter-embedded (calloc'd, untagged, API ' ') thread state. Debug build aborts via _PyMem_DebugRawFree bad ID; ft_release has the check compiled out and segfaults from the corrupted heap (captured mid-fault at _PyIndexPool_AllocIndex / new_threadstate pystate.c:1671). Fix: set interp->_initial_thread.base.interp = interp before publishing it as preallocated, or make free_threadstate identify the embedded tstate without trusting tstate->base.interp. Minimal repro drives _interpreters.create(reqrefs=True) directly; deterministic at start=31 (shipped repro.py, no args; 10/10) and start=30 (argv-driven sweep / original vehicle) on ft_debug_asan. repro_start (31) is sensitive to the script's allocation preamble (the argv/import variant hits it at 30); the defect itself is index-agnostic. Six vehicles all create a sub-interpreter under the OOM sweep; matched on 'bad ID' + Py_NewInterpreterFromConfig C stack.
